steps.title: Recognize what 'universal' requires to be weakened
steps.content: The student's claim relies on the word “universal”—asserting that all cultures use ego-based (egocentric) time concepts relative to their body orientation. One counterexample of a culture using a non-egocentric system would weaken this.

steps.title: Identify the counterexample
steps.content: C shows Yupno speakers pointing to a fixed geographical direction (uphill)—and crucially, regardless of which way they face, meaning their reference frame is not body-relative but fixed to the landscape. This non-egocentric system directly contradicts the student's claim that ego-based time is universal. A leaves the reference frame ambiguous; B addresses grammar, not conceptualization; D compares gesture frequency, not system type. That matches choice C — C is correct.
